commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is maintainer approval of the compatibility and security-boundary semantics for the new Policy grammar.; Cleared: No supply-chain, dependency, secret, script, or permission change was found; the remaining security concern is semantic Policy boundary review rather than a concrete patch defect. commands.review_summary:
Adds Policy plugin support for named `scopes.<scopeName>` overlays with `agentIds` selectors for `tools` and `agents.workspace`, plus exact `tools.alsoAllow.expected` conformance docs and tests.

PR surface: Source +859, Tests +873, Docs +253. Total +1985 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new config-conformance capability rather than a bug report. The behavior path is source-reviewable through the new scoped Policy evaluation code and focused tests.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is maintainer approval of the compatibility and security-boundary semantics for the new Policy grammar.

target.title: Policy: add agent-scoped policy overlays
target.body:
## Summary

Implements Policy overlays under scopes.<scopeName> for sections whose evidence is attributable to runtime agent ids: tools and agents.workspace.

Each named scope lists runtime agent ids with agentIds. The scope name is descriptive only; matching uses normalized OpenClaw agent ids. Scope blocks reuse the normal policy section grammar instead of a per-agent mini-language.

The overlay model is additive and config-conformance only. Top-level tools and agents.workspace claims still run, while scoped tools and agents.workspace blocks can add stricter claims and emit their own findings. Unsupported scoped sections are rejected rather than ignored. This PR does not add runtime enforcement.

Strictness metadata now validates scoped overlays: allowlist-subset, denylist-superset, requires-true, requires-false, and exact-list. tools.denyTools uses existing group and glob coverage semantics.

Example shape: scopes.release-workspace.agentIds targets release-agent and support-agent for agents.workspace; scopes.release-tools.agentIds targets release-agent for stricter tools.exec and tools.alsoAllow policy.

## Verification

- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plan/policy-agent-scoped-overlays.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md
- pnpm exec oxlint extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts
- pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md docs/plan/policy-agent-scoped-overlays.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=30000 -> 2 files, 183 tests passed
- git diff --check
- codex review --base origin/main -> no discrete/actionable regressions after accepting the main-agent fallback finding

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Policy conformance can express stricter workspace and tool posture claims for named policy scopes that target explicit runtime agent ids without weakening deployment-wide policy claims.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 checkout under /root/src, using Policy doctor and CLI Vitest coverage for the policy check and evaluation path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=30000

Evidence after fix: focused tests cover scopes.<scopeName> shape validation, scoped agents.workspace and tools findings, overlapping scopes, stricter-overlay validation, disabled empty allowlist semantics, tools.denyTools group coverage, implicit main fallback matching, and unsupported-section rejection for the agentIds selector.

Observed result after fix: 2 files and 183 tests passed; docs and formatter/linter checks also passed.

What was not tested: live channel, sandbox, ingress, or runtime tool enforcement. This PR intentionally adds Policy conformance and audit grammar, not runtime enforcement.
target.author: giodl73-repo
